title: Irrv
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[General/Legends|general]]|[[Dimone Irrv]], the [[Tagge Company/Legends|TaggeCo]] manager on [[Goroth Prime/Legends|Goroth Prime]]|linebreak=1}} {{Conflicting|"[[The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers]]"|[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 31|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 31]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Irrv |homeworld= |birth= |death=Between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[9 BBY/Legends|9 BBY]]Irrv's [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[insurrection on Culroon III]] as portrayed in "[[The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers]]" takes place following the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which took place in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' states that, following the insurrection, [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]] [[Reproduction/Legends|had]] a [[Parent/Legends|son]], [[Zevulon Veers]], and that Zevulon was a teenager in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Therefore, Zevulon must have been born by [[9 BBY/Legends|9 BBY]] at the latest, meaning that the insurrection on [[Culroon III/Legends|Culroon III]] must have taken place prior to or during that [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{FactFile|31|VEE 1-2|General Maximilian Veers}} |pronouns=He/him{{WEGCite|story=The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers|book=The Star Wars Sourcebook}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|General, I do believe you owe me an apology.''"
"''What I owe you, Lieutenant Veers, is a court-martial for disobeying my direct…|Lieutenant Veers and General Irrv, the latter of whom was interupted by a shot from Commander Grath|}} '''Irrv''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He rose to his position through his support of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], and was assigned by the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] to subjugate the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Culroon III/Legends|Culroon III]]. Irrv chose to coordinate a peaceful transition of power from the [[Culroon]] leader, [[Kloff]], through a ceremony, an idea that was decried by [[Junior Lieutenant/Legends|Junior Lieutenant]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]]. For speaking out, Irrv gave Veers a demerit, confined him to the [[Imperial garrison (Culroon III)|Imperial base]], and proceeded with the plan. The ceremony was attended by Irrv, the chief [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]], and a [[Legion/Legends|legion]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. When Irrv handed Kloff a [[Naval sword]], the Culroon stabbed his [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jeffers]], and thousands of Culroon [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] emerged from the surrounding [[Tree/Legends|trees]] to [[Insurrection on Culroon III|attack]] the Imperials, intending to kidnap the general. Irrv ordered the stormtroopers to form a protective circle around him until Veers intervened by marching an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] toward the ceremonial grounds, breaking the Culroon's attack. Irrv still threatened Veers with a [[Court-martial/Legends|court-martial]], but was [[Death/Legends|executed]] by [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|stormtrooper commander]] [[Grath (stormtrooper commander)|Grath]] first. ==Biography== ===Early career and occupation of Culroon III=== {{Quote|We might attain this planet with a show of force and by spilling a little Culroon blood, but not with a hollow ceremony.''"
"''Nonsense, Lieutenant! Your Academy training has made you a tad bloodthirsty, that's all. One more word, Veers, and I'll have you riding a dewback on Tatooine!|Veers and Irrv|}} [[File:Planet10-SWR.png|thumb|right|250px|Culroon III, which Irrv was ordered to subjugate by Emperor Palpatine]] Irrv was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] who supported [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]]{{Film|III}} [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] as it supplanted the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' As a result, Irrv was guaranteed a high [[Military rank/Legends|military rank]] and became a [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] of the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Between the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the Empire and [[9 BBY/Legends|9 BBY]], Irrv was ordered by Palpatine to subjugate the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Culroon III/Legends|Culroon III]] and its indigenous [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Culroon]], in whatever manner he saw fit. The general hoped to understand and work with the Culroon,{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|4|Welcome On Board}} and therefore he arranged to hold a ceremony with the Culroon's leader, [[Kloff]], to peacefully transfer power to the Empire through the exchange of a ceremonial [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and a [[Naval sword]]. The [[Junior Lieutenant/Legends|Junior Lieutenant]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]] spent several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] supervising the construction of an [[Imperial garrison (Culroon III)|Imperial garrison]] in Kloff's territory. In that [[Time/Legends|time]], Veers learned about the brutal [[Culroon wars|wars]] that occurred throughout the species' history and believed they would not surrender peacefully. At a staff meeting, Veers criticized the planned ceremony, unaware that his superior [[Officer/Legends|officer]] had orchestrated it, and suggested that the Imperials use force to subjugate the Culroon. Irrv accused Veers of bloodlust stemming from his training at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] and threatened to reassign him to riding a [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]] on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] if he continued to speak. ===The failed ceremony=== {{Quote|Major Veers, you'll be good enough to escort us back to the garrison?|Commander Grath promotes Veers after executing General Irrv|}} [[File:Veers-hd.png|thumb|left|285px|Lieutenant Maximilian Veers (pictured) saved General Irrv during the insurrection on Culroon III.]] Irrv gave Veers a demerit for speaking out and confined him to the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] for the duration of the Imperial occupation, under threat of [[Court-martial/Legends|court-martial]] if he disobeyed. Irrv attended the ceremony alongside the other chief [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] on the planet, including his [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jeffers]], and [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|stormtrooper commander]] [[Grath (stormtrooper commander)|Grath]], who was Irrv's superior officer.{{CSWECite|2|117|Irrv, General}} The officers were accompanied by a [[Legion/Legends|legion]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] wielding ceremonial [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]]. Kloff attended the ceremony with only a dozen [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]], while he had thousands secretly hidden in the surrounding [[Tree/Legends|trees]], ready to launch an [[Insurrection on Culroon III|insurrection]]. When Irrv handed Kloff the Naval [[Sword/Legends|sword]], the Culroon calmly stabbed Jeffers with it, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him.{{CSWECite|2|153|Jeffers}} At that signal, the Culroon warriors emerged and began attacking the Imperials, with their objective being to capture Irrv. Irrv ordered the stormtroopers to form a protective circle around him, which was weakened through Kloff's superior numbers and his warrior's power weapons. The surviving Imperials were saved through the intervention of Veers, who had been operating an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] at the garrison and marched it toward the ceremony site. Grath rallied the stormtroopers to safety beneath the [[Walker/Legends|walker]] while the Culroon realized their blaster-fire was ineffective against it and fled. Once the Culroon had retreated, Veers looked down at Irrv and stated that the general owed him an apology. Irrv's skin reddened as he began to threaten Veers with a court-martial, but before he could finish his sentence, Grath shot and killed him. The stormtrooper [[Commander/Legends|commander]] stepped over Irrv's corpse{{FactFile|45|VEE 3-4|General Maximilian Veers}} and promoted Veers to the rank of [[Major/Legends|major]] for his actions. ==Personality and traits== Irrv's loyalty to the New Order guaranteed him a position of authority, although the role was beyond his skill. Even with Palpatine's permission to claim their planet through any means necessary, Irrv was willing to understand and work with the Culroon. Veers was irritated by Irrv's command, perceiving him as an "old school" officer and fool. Irrv lashed out when Veers criticized his ideas at a staff meeting and threatened the junior lieutenant with reassignment. While under attack, Irrv ordered the legion of stormtroopers to form a protective circle around him. Even after Veers saved Irrv and the other Imperials from the Culroon, the general visibly reddened and was ready to court-martial Veers until Grath's lethal intervention. ==Equipment== During the ceremony on Culroon III, General Irrv and the other officers wore [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial dress uniforms]]. ==Behind the scenes== General Irrv first appeared in the short story "[[The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers]]," published in [[West End Games]]' [[1987]] ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'' written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Curtis Smith]]. The [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 31|thirty-first issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine, written by [[Iain Lowson]]{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}} and published on [[July 31]], [[2002]],{{FFdates|31}} stated that in the ceremony on Culroon III, Kloff was to give the Naval sword to Irrv in exchange for a ceremonial blaster, whereas in "The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers," Irrv hands the Naval sword to Kloff. The contradiction was repeated in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 14|fourteenth issue]] of the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File (2014)|revised run]] of ''The Official Star Wars Fact File'',{{FactFile2014|14|VEE 1-4|General Maximilian Veers}} which was published on [[April 9]], [[2014]]{{FactFile2014Dates|14}} and also authored by Lowson. This article follow's the short story's version of events. ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=The Promotion of Lieutenant Veers|book=The Star Wars Sourcebook|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|31|VEE 1-2|General Maximilian Veers}} *{{FactFile|45|VEE 3-4|General Maximilian Veers}} *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|4|Welcome On Board}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|generalmaximilianveers|Veers, General Maximilian}} *{{FactFile2014|14|VEE 1-4|General Maximilian Veers}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Irrv }} [[Category:Imperial Army generals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
